content: Acute flare treatment (start within 24 hours): NSAIDs (indomethacin, naproxen, celecoxib); colchicine 1.2mg then 0.6mg 1 hour later, then 0.6mg daily-bid (lower doses than historical); glucocorticoids (prednisone 30-40mg daily × 5-10 days, or intra-articular). IL-1 inhibitors (anakinra) for refractory cases. Urate-lowering therapy (ULT) indications: ≥2 flares/year, tophi, CKD stage ≥3, urolithiasis, serum urate >9mg/dL. First-line: allopurinol (xanthine oxidase inhibitor) start 100mg/day (50mg if CKD), titrate every 2-5 weeks to target urate <6mg/dL (<5mg/dL if tophaceous); max 800mg/day. Febuxostat alternative (cardiovascular safety concerns). Probenecid (uricosuric) if eGFR >50. Pegloticase (recombinant uricase) for refractory severe gout. Flare prophylaxis when starting ULT: colchicine 0.6mg daily-bid, low-dose NSAID, or prednisone ≤10mg/day for ≥6 months. Lifestyle: weight loss, limit alcohol/purines, avoid fructose beverages.
